entities.name: SPEED BREEDING
entities.description: A technique to accelerate the rate of plant improvement by reducing the generation interval through controlled environments.

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL BREEDING
entities.description: Traditional crop breeding methods that are time and resource intensive.

entities.name: SEED-TO-SEED CYCLE
entities.description: The duration from planting a seed to the harvest of the next generation of seeds.

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD EXTENSION
entities.description: The practice of extending light exposure to accelerate plant growth.

entities.name: TEMPERATURE CONTROL
entities.description: The regulation of temperature to optimize plant growth cycles.

entities.name: EARLY SEED HARVEST
entities.description: The practice of harvesting seeds as soon as they are viable to shorten the cycle.

entities.name: LONG-DAY PLANTS
entities.description: Plants that flower when the day length exceeds a certain threshold.

entities.name: SHORT-DAY PLANTS
entities.description: Plants that flower when the day length is shorter than a certain threshold.

entities.name: GENERATION INTERVAL TIME
entities.description: The time elapsed between the sowing of one generation and the sowing of the next.

entities.name: POPULATION DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The creation of genetic populations for research and breeding.

entities.name: GENOMIC SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding method using genomic information to predict plant performance.

entities.name: PHENOTYPING
entities.description: The observation and measurement of physical traits in plants.

entities.name: GENOMIC EDITING
entities.description: The use of technology to make precise changes to the plant genome.

entities.name: TRAIT PYRAMIDING
entities.description: The process of combining multiple desirable traits into a single plant variety.

entities.name: FOOD CROPS
entities.description: Agricultural crops grown primarily for human consumption.
